description: Create a scale model of the orbits of Mars and Earth in GeoGebra using Kepler's First Law and NASA/JPL data. Position the Sun at the origin (0,0). Model Earth's orbit as a circle with radius 1 AU centered at the Sun. Model Mars's orbit as an ellipse with the Sun at one focus, using a semi-major axis (a) of 1.524 AU and eccentricity (e) of 0.0934. The model must include labeled 'Perihelion' and 'Aphelion' points on Mars's orbit and a text label displaying the eccentricity value (e = 0.0934). Save the project as 'mars_orbit.ggb' to ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/.
